title: Columnea eburnea
intro: Columnea eburnea, also known as the ivory-flowered Columnea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Columnea eburnea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Columnea eburnea is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health ailments in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Columnea eburnea is classified within the family Gesneriaceae. This family comprises various species of flowering plants native to Central and South America. Columnea eburnea is a species of the genus Columnea, which includes approximately 100 species of flowering plants. The plant is further classified as belonging to the subfamily Gesnerioideae within the Gesneriaceae family. Further classification includes the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the mint order.
